title: Print post-order traversal
content:
Given a binary tree, print its items in a postorder traversial
Example 0
PostOrderPrint(Tree(1,385,45,12,7,235,8)) -> (12, 7, 385, 235, 8, 45, 1)
Example 1
PostOrderPrint(Tree(25, 12, 7, 23, 8, 1, 34)) -> (1, 8, 7, 23, 12, 34, 25)

comments.author: 3fa7a504-89fe-46f7-bb93-80ef40dd1671
comments.content: Has no one solved this question yet?

author: 3fa7a504-89fe-46f7-bb93-80ef40dd1671
title: Print in-order traversial
content:
Print the in-order traversial of a BST.
Example 0
PrintInOrder((1, 385, 45, 12, 7, 235, 8)) -> (1, 7, 8, 12, 45, 235, 385)
Example 1
PrintInOrder((25, 12, 7, 23, 8, 1, 34)) -> (1, 7, 8, 12, 23, 25, 34)
